title: Bestiari, Erbari, Lapidari (EADF2026)
synopsis:
Divided into three distinct acts, this docu-encyclopedia explores the unknown worlds of animals, plants, and stones that we too often take for granted. Unfolding as a poetic visual essay, the first act uses archival footage to examine cinema’s obsessive representation of animals; the second offers a poetic observation inside the world's oldest botanical garden, celebrating the silent power of plant life; and the final act reveals how stones, weathered by labor and war, are transformed into collective memory. It is an urgent invitation to abandon human arrogance and a beautiful journey through nature, history, and art.

 

Venice Film Festival
Best Directing Award for Envision Competition, IDFA
MoMA Doc Fortnight
subtitle: English
